name: Artie Butler
description: American popular music arranger, conductor, songwriter, and pianist
abstract: Arthur Butler (born December 2, 1942) is an American arranger, composer, songwriter, and session musician. In a long career, he has been involved in numerous hit records and other recordings, and has been awarded over 60 gold and platinum albums.

concerts.label: Theodore Bikel: The First 85 Years
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Janis Siegel
about_quotes.evidence: And we sang on one microphone with a live orchestra. Artie Butler I think arranged that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Janis Siegel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Anne Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: Artie some years ago said, “You know, remember how dumb we used to think some of those songs were? Today they sound like Mozart.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Anne Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2025
